# Symbiotic AI - Project Identity
You are an expert Frontend Architect specializing in Deep Tech and AI Safety.
Our mission: Deterministic Governance for the Agentic Era.

## Design System:
- Theme: Dark Mode (Space/Tech Aesthetic).
- Palette: Deep Blacks (#050505), Cyan Accents (#00FFFF), Glassmorphism cards.
- Library: Tailwind CSS.

## Key Components for Investors:
- Live Sentry Status: Visual indicators of the Azure/AWS link.
- Trace Logs: A mock (or live) scrolling log of "Reasoning Traces."
- Investor CTA: A high-contrast "Secure a Pilot" button.

## The "Investor Landing Page" Prompt
The AI chat in VS Code ($Ctrl+I$ or $Cmd+I$) should use this exact prompt to generate your core page:

"Scaffold a professional landing page for symbiotic-ai.tech using Tailwind CSS. Hero Section: Headline: 'The Sentry for Multi-Cloud Intelligence.' Sub-headline: 'Deterministic AI Governance and TRiSM for the Enterprise.' The 'Double Green' Section: Create a visual grid showing two cards: 'AWS Bedrock Node' (Status: Active). 'Azure Cosmos DB Memory' (Status: Synced). The Proof Section: A glass-morphic code block that simulates a 'Reasoning Trace' JSON output from our CMIS Node. Links: Add a footer with 'Live Demo', 'Documentation', and 'Investor Inquiry'."

## Deploying for the Future (SWA CLI)
Since you've already looked at the SWA CLI docs, don't just "run" it. Use the CLI to ensure your domain is investor-ready.

Run these commands in your terminal to sync your local code with the domain permanently:

```powershell
# 1. Login to your corporate Azure account
swa login

# 2. Link your code to your existing 'Symbiotic-site'
swa init

# 3. Deploy to Production (This removes the "Waiting for deployment" status)
swa deploy --env production
```

## The "Demo Link" Strategy
Investors don't want to see your code; they want to see your governance in action.

Ask the AI to build a "Demo Dashboard" route:

"Create a new page /demo. It should have a split screen. On the left: a prompt input. On the right: three output boxes: 1. 'Raw LLM Response', 2. 'CMIS Governance Filter', 3. 'Final Secured Output'. This will prove to investors that our Sentry actually works."

## The Vision Check:
By doing this, you aren't just making a "site." You are building a SaaS Interface. When you show symbiotic-ai.tech to an investor, they see a company that has already solved the hardest part of AI: Control.

Should I generate the HTML for that 'Reasoning Trace' JSON preview so it looks beautiful on the landing page, or are you ready to run the swa deploy command?
